perturbation.original_text: DGT shall pay Dolphin USD10,000, for carrying out the beta testing of the quality of their photo-editing services and testing of the Internet bandwidth for file transmission. This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments, USD5,000 on the date of this Agreement and USD5,000, 30 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: DGT shall pay Dolphin USD10,000, for carrying out the beta testing of the quality of their photo-editing services and testing of the Internet bandwidth for file transmission. This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments, USD2,500 on the date of this Agreement, USD2,500, 30 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement, USD2,500, 60 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement, and USD2,500, 90 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: The change in payment terms for the beta testing fee creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, payment was structured in two installments of USD5,000 each. The modification dictates four installments of USD2,500 each. This directly contradicts the original payment schedule, generating confusion regarding the amount and timing of payments for the beta testing phase. Specifically, this contradicts with the section that mentions 'This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DGT shall pay Dolphin USD10,000, for carrying out the beta testing of the quality of their photo-editing services and testing of the Internet bandwidth for file transmission. This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments, USD2,500 on the date of this Agreement, USD2,500, 30 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement, USD2,500, 60 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement, and USD2,500, 90 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment schedule for the beta testing fee, changing it from two installments of USD5,000 each to four installments of USD2,500 each. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction aligns with the definition of inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Dolphin agrees to complete its photo-editing services within 14 days of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT pre-defined standards for finished products
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin agrees to complete its photo-editing services within 7 days of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT pre-defined standards for finished products
perturbation.explanation: The change shortens the turnaround time for photo-editing services from 14 days to 7 days. This alteration creates an in-text contradiction with any subsequent clauses that might implicitly or explicitly rely on the original 14-day timeframe. It is contradicting the time agreed previously for services rendered. Also, any agreement or time-frame agreed in the other section, will cause confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon successful completion of the beta testing stage, DGT will guarantee Dolphin the greater of: (1) USD5,000 for each 30 day period of photo-editing work completed for a total of 90 days (payment to be effective 30 days from the successful completion of the beta tests); or (2) a minimum of 3,300 pictures to be edited for each 30 day period for a total of 90 days at USD1.50 per finished digital photo;
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a 14-day completion timeframe for photo-editing services, upon which other clauses rely. The changed text reduces this timeframe to 7 days. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which defines payment terms based on a 30-day period of work and also with the number of pictures that should be edited in 30 days. If Dolphin can only process photos for 7 days instead of 14 as previously agreed, this affects the timeframe agreed for payment and volume of edited photos. Therefore, the reduction in the completion timeframe from 14 to 7 days directly contradicts the payment terms based on 30-day periods, creating an in-text inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 30 days of the notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 15 days of the notice.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the period allowed for DGT to rectify a failure before Dolphin can terminate the agreement creates a conflict. Initially, DGT had 30 days to rectify the failure. The modification reduces this period to 15 days. This directly contradicts the original timeframe, leading to ambiguity and potential disputes over the termination rights and obligations of Dolphin. If DGT were to rely on the initial 30 days, it would be in breach of contract according to the modified clause, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over the enforceability of the termination terms. This contradiction arises from the reduction of the grace period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement if Dolphin fails to meet its requirements and material obligation hereunder and only after DGT have given Dolphin written notice of such failure and only after Dolphin has not rectified such failure within 10 days of the notice. Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 15 days of the notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the time period for DGT to rectify a failure from 30 days to 15 days. The contradicted text repeats the changed text and refers to the same issue of DGT failing to meet a material obligation and not rectifying such failure. Because the grace periods for rectification are different, the amended clause creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding the time allowed for DGT to rectify a failure, leading to uncertainty over the applicable timeframe and potentially impacting termination rights and obligations.
